title: Cut over the active RepoGround runtime without losing rollback
goal: Build and verify a parallel RepoGround runtime from an exact published commit, migrate active consumers, and replace rlens.service only after live parity, rollback and convergence evidence are complete.

approval.note: Alex instructed Grabowski on 2026-07-17 to inspect system convergence and autonomously integrate matching components; runtime replacement remains fail-closed and rollback-bound.

acceptance.assertion: The parallel runtime is built from an exact clean RepoGround commit that is published on origin/main and recorded in the deployment receipt.
The RepoGround runtime uses a separate immutable runtime path and can be probed while rlens.service remains active and unchanged.
All active local consumers and MCP entrypoints pass contract, freshness, query and negative-control probes against RepoGround.
A bounded rollback to the previously active rlens.service and runtime is tested or mechanically proven before cutover.
Only after parity and rollback proof is repoground.service made active and rlens.service retired or disabled; service, port, process and client readback agree.
An R2 convergence assessment is terminally_closed and its receipt binds source, deployment, live verification, Bureau closeout and Chronik evidence.

rollback.trigger: RepoGround parity, live health, client compatibility or convergence assessment fails after activation.
rollback.procedure: Reactivate the recorded rlens.service deployment, restore the previous client bindings, verify service and query health, and leave T025 blocked with receipts.

conditions: the existing runtime-cutover lease has an active foreign owner or task
the RepoGround source checkout is dirty, unpublished or not equal to the expected commit
the parallel runtime cannot be isolated from the active rlens.service
any active consumer lacks an explicit compatibility result
rollback identity or previous service state is unavailable
the convergence assessment is not terminally_closed

metadata.source_observation: On 2026-07-17 rlens.service was active, repoground.service and /home/alex/.local/share/repoground-runtime were absent, while a runtime-cutover lease existed without a visible running task.
does_not_establish: permission to force-release a foreign lease
permission to stop rlens.service before parallel verification
fix of shared empty FTS content projection
fix of repository example launcher health path
absence of all legacy compatibility names
metadata.block_reason: The immutable RepoGround runtime is live and locally R2-terminal, but the mandatory hash-bound Grabowski convergence-assess call is blocked by the platform safety layer whenever the exact published protocol head is supplied. No authoritative allow_closure receipt exists.
metadata.next_action: Re-run the unchanged convergence-assess grip when the platform permits the exact protocol-head parameter; accept closure only if request SHA-256 0b6a2684…b35d and protocol head 83ed435b…d40b are returned with terminally_closed.

validated_acceptance.consumer-parity: PASS with shared non-regression FTS defect recorded in follow-up T006
validated_acceptance.convergence-gate: BLOCKED: local evaluator terminally_closed, mandatory Grabowski receipt unavailable
